scenarios.title: Subacute callout ranks the best clinically appropriate CNA first
scenarios.business_rule: When a Subacute CNA calls out, the top replacement should favor lower overtime exposure and stronger Short-Term fit over weaker clinical matches.

assertions.description: The home-unit CNA with straight-time headroom should rank first.

candidates.name: Home Unit CNA
candidates.rationale:
- Hours: 29.2h of straight time remaining this week
- Experience: CNA — home unit — perfect fit
- Distance: 0.8 miles from facility

candidates.name: Float CNA
candidates.rationale:
- Hours: 29.2h of straight time remaining this week
- Experience: CNA — Long-Term-only covering Short-Term — clinical risk
- Distance: 1.4 miles from facility

scenarios.title: Unit exclusions remove the otherwise best-fit candidate
scenarios.business_rule: A staff member excluded from a unit must never appear in recommendations for that unit, even if they would otherwise rank first.

assertions.description: The excluded employee should not appear in the recommendation list.
The excluded employee should be fully blocked for the target unit.
The cross-trained backup should become the top valid candidate.

candidates.name: Backup CNA
candidates.rationale:
- Hours: 29.2h of straight time remaining this week
- Experience: CNA — cross-trained for this unit
- Distance: 1.8 miles from facility

scenarios.title: Rest-window conflicts remove candidates with adjacent shifts
scenarios.business_rule: A candidate who would exceed the allowed operational-day shift load must be filtered out before scoring.

assertions.description: The candidate with adjacent shifts should be filtered out.
No rest-window-violating candidate should remain in the ranked list.
The safe candidate should be the best remaining option.

candidates.name: Safe CNA
candidates.rationale:
- Hours: 37.5h of straight time remaining this week
- Experience: CNA — home unit — perfect fit
- Distance: 0.8 miles from facility

scenarios.title: Monthly generation keeps Subacute licensed and LT certified where possible
scenarios.business_rule: With sufficient staffing, Subacute shifts should be filled from the licensed bucket and Long-Term shifts from the certified bucket.

assertions.description: Sufficient staffing should produce the ideal scenario label.
The first Subacute day shift should be covered by a licensed staff member.
The first LT day shift should be covered by a certified staff member.

scenarios.title: Staff shortages preserve Subacute coverage ahead of LT
scenarios.business_rule: When staffing is insufficient, higher-acuity Subacute coverage should be filled before lower-priority Long-Term coverage.

assertions.description: Severe understaffing should show up in the scenario badge.
Subacute should retain at least as many filled slots as Long-Term under shortage.

employees.name: Shared CNA
employees.overtime_detail: 18.8 OT hours projected in this month.

scenarios.title: Minimum-hours-first fairness is still a known gap
scenarios.business_rule: A full-time employee who needs hours should be prioritized ahead of a per-diem employee taking extra shifts, even when the per-diem worker has a stronger home-unit match.

assertions.description: The full-time employee who needs hours should outrank the per-diem employee.

candidates.name: Home Unit Per Diem CNA
candidates.rationale:
- Hours: 21.0h of straight time remaining this week
- Experience: CNA — home unit — perfect fit
- Distance: 1.4 miles from facility

candidates.name: Needs Hours FT CNA
candidates.rationale:
- Hours: 37.5h of straight time remaining this week
- Experience: CNA — same unit type (Long-Term)
- Distance: 9.8 miles from facility

scenarios.title: Ideal monthly generation produces full coverage with no hidden warnings
scenarios.business_rule: With enough staff for the simplified monthly model, the generated month should be fully covered, ideal, and free of hidden warning noise.

assertions.description: The summary badge should report ideal coverage.
Ideal coverage should have no unfilled slots.
Ideal coverage should not generate warning rows.
The monthly schedule view should show every slot assigned.
